vitics_knowledge_base.platform: Vitics — Nigeria AI Energy Intelligence Platform

entries.title: Nigeria Integrated Energy Plan (IEPT) — Full Corpus

source.publisher: SEforALL, Rockefeller Foundation, Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet

programs.job_creation_target: 250,000 new jobs in solar value chain
programs.nigeria_electrification_project: REA collaboration with World Bank and AfDB for mini-grid priority areas

clean_cooking.lpg_clean_cooking_target: 2 million MT from clean cooking by 2027
clean_cooking.cbn_intervention_facility: NGN 250 billion for gas expansion program

productive_use.target: Electrify rice and maize milling in unelectrified agricultural settlements
productive_use.mechanism: Productive use demand reduces mini-grid cost per residential connection by sharing costs with milling operations
productive_use.projected_impact: ~200,000 additional mini-grid connections; ~3,389 new mini-grid settlements

electrification_access_gap.note_on_rate: Facilities designated as electrified may only be partially or under-electrified — official rate likely overstates reliable access

affordability_crisis.geographic_note: Affordability gap concentrated in Northern Nigeria — highest density of unelectrified households

households_with_no_clean_cooking_option.note: Cannot afford or are not likely to adopt any clean cooking technology under current conditions

clean_cooking_cost_comparison.note: Annual cooking fuel cost per household
clean_cooking_cost_comparison.insight: Charcoal at NGN 6,589/year remains price-competitive vs all clean alternatives, driving continued use despite health and climate harms

grid_reliability_gap.policy_admission: The plan explicitly recommends 'increasing hours of supply and reliability of supply on grid to ensure that power is available during mealtimes for e-cooking' — confirming current grid is insufficient even for connected households

productive_use_constraint.farmland_proximity_issue: 92% of farmlands are more than 1 mile from community centers — beyond viable mini-grid distribution radius for irrigation
productive_use_constraint.cold_chain_viability: Limited — consumer preference for fresh catfish (70% of farmed fish) and low milk yields make cold storage unviable at scale
productive_use_constraint.viable_productive_use: Rice and maize milling only, concentrated in Northern and Central states

adoption_barrier.clean_cooking_model_sensitivity: Highly sensitive to adoption assumptions — base case is only 10-12%; aggressive adoption scenario could yield 13–22 million households
adoption_barrier.key_barrier: Female adult education used as adoption proxy — social, cultural, and gender factors exclude millions even where technology and affordability criteria are met

time_savings.applies_to: All three clean cooking technologies equally

ai_engine_context.document_type: Government planning document with embedded gap analysis
ai_engine_context.consumer_experience_framing: The IEPT is a forward-looking model, not an outcomes report. Consumer reality is extracted from: (1) affordability gap admissions, (2) institutional electrification data, (3) the 88% exclusion rate from clean cooking, and (4) the plan's own recommendations about grid reliability — which presuppose current unreliability.
ai_engine_context.key_tension: The plan models what is technically possible at least cost. It does not model whether the funding, political will, or institutional capacity exists to execute. The gap between USD 25.8 billion needed and USD 288 million committed is the central consumer reality.
vitics_use_cases: Benchmark actual grid reliability data against the IEPT electrification rate claims
Map affordability gap geographically — Northern Nigeria households most at risk
Track LPG demand growth against 5 million MT government target
Monitor NEP grant disbursements vs USD 288 million commitment
Assess clean cooking adoption trends against 10-12% base case projection
